creator: ohartmann@walstatt.org
text:
What ever I configure, I receive a 

Abor trap
===> Options unchanged


I downgraded with ports-mgmt/portdowngrade to subversion1-1.7.10, but
now I see the same there: rejected options. The dialog looks ugly (like
the new option facility), repeating two times the selection always with
selections resetted to inital values.

Since we use Apache 2.4 and I receive a lot of obscure errors like 
 
make: "/usr/ports/Mk/bsd.apache.mk" line 284: warning:
"/usr/local/sbin/apxs -q MPM_NAME" returned non-zero status

I found this in /usr/ports/Mk/bsd.apache.mk:

[...]
# XXX see mod_perl-2.0.6/Changes
# Apache 2.4 and onwards doesn't require linking the MPM module
# directly in the httpd binary anymore. APXS lost the MPM_NAME query,
# so we can't assume a given MPM anymore.
.       if ${_APACHE_VERSION} <= 22
APACHE_MPM!=            ${APXS} -q MPM_NAME
.       endif
.elif defined(APACHE_PORT)
_APACHE_VERSION!=       ${ECHO_CMD} ${APACHE_PORT} | ${SED} -ne
's,.*/apache\([0-9]*\).*,\1,p' .endif

I do not understand this. It seems that the port build framework
already takes care of Apache 2.4, but why is then this linke in
bsd.apache.mk run?
